text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a software development practice where team members merge their code changes frequently, often several times a day. Each code change is automatically built and tested through a CI pipeline. This means that as soon as a developer pushes new code, the system runs automated builds and executes a set of tests. The main purpose of CI is to catch issues early, before they become bigger problems. By running tests regularly, developers can quickly identify and fix bugs, integration conflicts, or other errors. This process helps maintain code quality, reduces risks, and makes teamwork smoother and more reliable.
